entity: Harvard Graduate School of Arts and Sciences
explicit_text: I attended the Harvard Graduate School of Arts and Sciences where I studied geology under Professor Hugh McKinstry. During my time there, I completed a Ph.D. thesis focused on using manganese oxide as a guide to ore in the Tintic District, Utah. Harvard was instrumental in shaping my academic and research skills.

explicit_text: From 1960 to 1967, I worked as an exploration geologist for Kennecott Copper Corporation. During this period, I developed new procedures for copper exploration and researched leached cappings, traveling extensively in search of deposits. This role was my first major professional experience in the mining industry.

explicit_text: In 1981, the Solano Irrigation District became involved with the McLaughlin Mine due to concerns about water quality in the Berryessa watershed. Betsy Marchand alerted us to potential impacts, and we pushed for strict oversight including fail-safe tailings containment and daily monitoring. Our vigilance helped protect Solano County’s water resources.
